name: Dominick Cruz

qualityWinLedger.context: Beat the reigning UFC bantamweight champion after a long injury layoff.
UFC bantamweight title win over an elite title challenger; later flyweight greatness is context, not back-credit.
Top-five bantamweight contender stopped immediately in Cruz’s return fight.
Elite UFC bantamweight title challenger in Cruz’s first UFC championship fight.
Ranked bantamweight contender win late in Cruz’s UFC career.
Veteran elite bantamweight title challenger, discounted for age and repeat-opponent context.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Garbrandt did not automatically end it; Cejudo title loss is the endpoint. Vera is post-prime.

lossContextEvents.context: Cruz later returned directly to title-level relevance against Henry Cejudo.

shortCase: Cruz is one of the smartest and most unique champions ever: brilliant footwork, elite fight IQ, a historic comeback, and a complicated resume shaped by injuries and gaps.
bestArgument: Cruz wins when the debate values tactical brilliance, comeback legacy, and peak skill over raw fight volume.
counterArgument: Cruz’s argument is uniqueness and comeback value. Few fighters ever returned from that much time away and beat a champion like Dillashaw.
whyRankedHere: Cruz ranks here because his best UFC work is brilliant. The Demetrious Johnson win, the Dillashaw comeback, and his unique championship style give him a real all-time bantamweight case inside the UFC scoring boundary.
whyNotHigher: He does not rank higher because this is UFC-only, the WEC reign is excluded, and injury gaps limit active elite years. Garbrandt and Cejudo are counted inside the UFC prime window.
finalTakeaway: Cruz is the injury-fragmented genius: one of the smartest and most unique champions ever, but UFC-only scoring keeps the total resume below the deeper title-volume cases.
